message:
feat(br-shfc): add flake-triage CLI integration tests

Add 17 integration tests for `am flake-triage` CLI:
- Flag parsing for scan/reproduce/detect subcommands
- Artifact scanning with fixtures (nested directories)
- Malformed artifact handling (skip with warning)
- JSON output schema validation
- Timestamp sorting (most recent first)
- Empty/nonexistent directory handling
- Help output verification

Also fixes 4 pre-existing compile errors in robot.rs:
- build_agents() signature (add active_only, sort_field)
- build_thread() signature (add since, include_bodies)
- CliError::NotFound → CliError::Other (variant doesn't exist)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -485,7 +485,7 @@
 {"id":"br-2bbt.12","title":"[track] Documentation Phase 1: Core features keybindings and runbook","description":"Update documentation to reflect TUI V2 changes:

1. Keybinding card update:
   - Add Ctrl+P: Command Palette (NOT / which conflicts with per-screen search bars)
     NOTE: Ctrl+K was DROPPED (conflicts with readline kill-to-end). Ctrl+P is the ONLY palette trigger.
   - Add . (period): Contextual action menu on selected item
   - Add Ctrl+T: Focus toast notification stack (NOT bare t which conflicts with per-screen keys)
   - Add Ctrl+S: Save filter preset (safe in raw-mode TUI; XOFF disabled)
   - Add Ctrl+L: Load filter preset
   - Add Space: Toggle item selection (batch mode)
   - Add Shift+A: Select all visible items
   - Add J (CAPITAL J): Toggle JSON tree view (NOT lowercase j which conflicts with help scroll + search nav)
   - Add Alt+Left: Back navigation (navigation stack)
   - Add Ctrl+N: Open compose panel
   - Add V (CAPITAL V): Events/Commits/Combined view toggle (Timeline screen only)
   - Add v: Enter/exit visual selection mode (batch screens)
   - Add g: Global/Local inbox toggle (Messages screen only)
   - Add n: Network graph toggle (Contacts screen only)

2. OPERATOR_RUNBOOK.md updates:
   - New section: Command Palette usage guide
   - New section: Toast notification configuration
   - New section: Filter presets
   - Updated: Screen descriptions with new widget capabilities

3. README.md TUI section:
   - Update keybinding table
   - Add command palette description
   - Add screenshot descriptions for new features

4. AGENTS.md TUI section:
   - Update screen count and descriptions
   - Add new env vars for toast configuration

## Acceptance Criteria
- All new keybindings documented with CORRECT keys (Ctrl+P not /, Ctrl+T not t, . not Enter, J not j, V not v for Timeline)
- Operator runbook covers all new features
- README and AGENTS.md updated","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:57.280011450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:14:21.568931126Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.12","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:52:57.280011450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.12","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T00:53:11.892739085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.13","title":"[track] Agent Communication Network Graph Visualization","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail/archive/network features an interactive vis.js network graph showing:
- Nodes (agents) with scaling based on message volume
- Edges (message flows) with directional arrows
- Physics-based layout
- Hover tooltips with sent/received counts

The TUI has a Contacts screen (#11) but shows a flat table, not a graph.

## Widget Reference
- ftui_extras::canvas (Braille/HalfBlock/Block modes)
- NOTE: Canvas is FEATURE-GATED behind the "canvas" feature flag in ftui_extras.
  Must enable this feature in Cargo.toml: ftui_extras = { features = ["canvas"] }
- Painter API provides: draw_line, draw_circle, draw_rect, draw_text

## Implementation Approach

1. Add a Network sub-view to the Contacts screen (toggle with n key):
   - Use ftui_extras::canvas with Braille mode for highest resolution
   - Draw agent nodes as circles with size proportional to message count
   - Draw directed edges (arrows) between communicating agents
   - Edge thickness proportional to message volume between pair
   - Color coding: green=active contact, yellow=pending, red=blocked

2. Data source: query agent_links + message counts from DB
   - Cache graph layout (recompute only on data change)
   - Simple force-directed layout (or fixed grid for <20 agents)

3. Interaction:
   - Arrow keys to select/highlight a node
   - Enter on node to deep link to Agents screen (via br-2bbt.8)
   - Display tooltip with sent/received counts near selected node
   - Zoom in/out with +/- keys (changes canvas scale)

4. Fallback: At Tiny terminal size, show tabular view instead of graph

## Acceptance Criteria
- Network graph renders agent communication patterns visually
- Node sizing reflects message volume
- Edge directionality shows message flow
- Works at Compact and Large terminal sizes (Tiny falls back to table)
- Canvas feature flag enabled in Cargo.toml
- Unit tests for layout algorithm and data aggregation
- Snapshot tests for graph rendering with sample data","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:40.650225653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:02:43.645953626Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contacts","frankentui","tui","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.13","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:40.650225653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.14","title":"[track] Unified Cross-Project Inbox View","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail provides a unified inbox across ALL projects with full-text search,
filter tabs, sorting, importance badges, thread grouping, and project context per message.

The TUI Messages screen only shows messages for one project at a time. Operators managing
multiple projects must switch between them manually. This is the single biggest capability
gap between web and TUI.

## EXISTING INFRASTRUCTURE NOTE

The Search screen already has a Scope facet: ScopeMode (Global/Project/Product). The Messages
screen does NOT have an equivalent — it always operates on one project. This track adds
a Global/Local toggle to the Messages screen specifically.

The product bus already has cross-project capabilities:
- search_messages_product, fetch_inbox_product, summarize_thread_product
These should be reused or extended rather than building from scratch.

## Implementation Approach

1. Add a new view mode to the Messages screen: Local (current) vs Global (cross-project)
   - Toggle with g key on Messages screen ONLY
   - In Global mode, add project column to message table
   - Global mode queries across all projects in the DB

2. Global search:
   - Search bar in Global mode searches all projects
   - Results show project context (slug) alongside message data
   - FTS query runs across all projects

3. Filters in Global mode:
   - All / Unread / Urgent tabs (same as webapp)
   - Filter by project
   - Sort by newest/oldest/sender/project

4. Performance: cursor-based pagination (50 per page), project metadata cached

## Acceptance Criteria
- g key toggles between Local and Global inbox views (Messages screen only)
- Global view shows messages from all projects with project column
- Search in Global mode returns results across all projects
- Pagination handles large result sets
- Unit tests for cross-project query construction
- E2E test: create 2 projects with messages, toggle Global, verify all visible","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:57.337667836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:27:07.318807339Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-project","inbox","tui","webapp-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:08:57.337667836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-2bbt.14.1","title":"Add cross-project query methods to DB layer","description":"Add database query methods that operate across all projects:

1. fetch_inbox_global(limit, offset, filters) -> Vec<MessageRow> with project_slug joined
2. count_unread_global(agent_filter) -> per-project unread counts
3. search_messages_global(query, scope, sort) -> Vec<MessageRow> with project context

IMPORTANT: Reuse existing product bus infrastructure where possible.
The product bus already has cross-project capabilities:
- search_messages_product: searches across linked projects
- fetch_inbox_product: fetches inbox across product-linked projects
- summarize_thread_product: summarizes threads across products

The Global inbox queries may be able to extend these existing methods or share
their cross-project JOIN logic. The key difference is that product bus filters
by product linkage, while Global inbox shows ALL projects unconditionally.
Refactor the shared JOIN logic into reusable helpers if the code paths overlap.

These methods query across all projects in the DB. They join messages with projects
table to include project_slug in results. They respect the same filtering, sorting,
and pagination as per-project queries.

## Tests
- Unit: fetch_inbox_global returns messages from 3 test projects
- Unit: count_unread_global returns correct per-project counts
- Unit: search_messages_global respects scope filter across projects
- Unit: pagination (offset/limit) works correctly across project boundaries
- Unit: verify shared logic with product bus queries (no duplication)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.089383755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:50:44.109487867Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-project","database","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.1","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.089383755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

1. fetch_inbox_global(limit, offset, filters) -> Vec<MessageRow> with project_slug joined
2. count_unread_global(agent_filter) -> per-project unread counts
3. search_messages_global(query, scope, sort) -> Vec<MessageRow> with project context

IMPORTANT: Reuse existing product bus infrastructure where possible.
The product bus already has cross-project capabilities:
- search_messages_product: searches across linked projects
- fetch_inbox_product: fetches inbox across product-linked projects
- summarize_thread_product: summarizes threads across products

The Global inbox queries may be able to extend these existing methods or share
their cross-project JOIN logic. The key difference is that product bus filters
by product linkage, while Global inbox shows ALL projects unconditionally.
Refactor the shared JOIN logic into reusable helpers if the code paths overlap.

These methods query across all projects in the DB. They join messages with projects
table to include project_slug in results. They respect the same filtering, sorting,
and pagination as per-project queries.

## Tests
- Unit: fetch_inbox_global returns messages from 3 test projects
- Unit: count_unread_global returns correct per-project counts

- Unit: pagination (offset/limit) works correctly across project boundaries

 {"id":"br-2bbt.14.2","title":"Add Global/Local mode toggle to Messages screen UI","description":"Modify the Messages screen to support Global and Local viewing modes:

1. Add mode state: enum InboxMode { Local(project_slug), Global }
2. g key toggles between Local and Global modes
3. Mode indicator in screen header: [Local: my-project] or [Global: all projects]
4. In Global mode:
   - Add project column (colored badge) to message table
   - Filter tabs (All/Unread/Urgent) filter across all projects
   - Sort dropdown adds By Project option
   - Search bar searches across all projects
5. In Local mode:
   - Behavior unchanged from current implementation
   - All existing per-project functionality preserved exactly

Transition: switching Global -> Local remembers last active project. Switching Local -> Global preserves current search/filter state.

## Tests
- Unit: mode toggle state machine (Local -> Global -> Local)
- Unit: Global mode adds project column to table schema
- Snapshot: Global mode header showing [Global: all projects]
- Snapshot: message table with project column badges in Global mode
- E2E: create 2 projects with messages, toggle g, verify cross-project results","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.284094811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:21:19.872444777Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cross-project","tui","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.14","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:20:52.284094811Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.14.2","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt.14.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:21:19.872406185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.15","title":"[track] Archive Browser with File Content Preview","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp at GET /mail/archive/browser/{project_slug} provides a two-panel layout:
directory tree on left, file content on right, with auto-detection for markdown/JSON/text.

The TUI has no equivalent way to browse the Git archive. Operators must use the CLI or shell.

## Implementation Approach

1. Add a new screen (the TUI already has 14 screens in tui_screens/, adding one more):
   - tui_screens/archive_browser.rs (new file — genuinely new functionality)
   - Register in tui_screens/mod.rs as new MailScreenId::ArchiveBrowser variant
   - Left pane: directory tree of archive
   - Right pane: file content preview

2. Directory tree:
   - Use ftui_widgets::tree::Tree widget (verified to exist in frankentui)
   - Directories expandable/collapsible with Enter
   - Show file count per directory
   - Sort: directories first, then files alphabetically

3. Content preview pane:
   - .json files: syntax-highlighted JSON (use JSON tree widget from br-2bbt.7 if available)
   - .md files: rendered markdown (ftui_extras::markdown already used in TUI)
   - Other: plain text with line numbers
   - Show file metadata (size, last modified) in header

4. Navigation:
   - Arrow keys to navigate tree
   - Enter to expand directory or select file
   - Tab to switch focus between tree and preview
   - Esc to go up one directory
   - / to search file names within current directory

5. Data source: read from Git archive on disk (STORAGE_ROOT/{slug}/.archive/)
   - Cache directory listings (invalidate on archive write events)
   - Lazy-load file contents (only load when selected)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Two-panel archive browser renders correctly
- Directory tree shows archive structure using ftui_widgets::Tree
- File selection shows content preview with appropriate rendering
- Navigation works (expand/collapse, file select, pane switch)
- Works at Compact and Large terminal sizes
- Unit tests for directory listing, content type detection
- Snapshot tests for tree and preview rendering","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:09:15.411410587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:28:29.346692073Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["archive","browser","tui","webapp-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.15","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:09:15.411410587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2bbt.16","title":"[track] Activity Feed with Git Commit Timeline","description":"## Rationale (from webapp parity)

The webapp provides two complementary timeline views:

1. GET /mail/archive/activity - Activity Feed showing:
   - Recent commits across all projects
   - Stats bar (total commits, insertions, deletions, unique authors)
   - Commit timeline with visual connector lines
   - Commit hash, author/sender, recipients, date
   - Clickable links to commit details

2. GET /mail/archive/timeline - Communication Timeline showing:
   - Mermaid gitGraph visualization
   - Interactive commit list with sender->recipients flow

The TUI Timeline screen (#9) shows MailEvents but does NOT show Git commit history. Adding commit timeline data would give operators a complete picture of both messaging activity and archive writes.

## Implementation Approach

1. Extend the existing Timeline screen with a view toggle:
   - Events view (current): MailEvents from ring buffer
   - Commits view (new): Git commits from archive
   - Combined view: interleaved events + commits chronologically

2. View toggle key: V (CAPITAL V)
   KEYBINDING NOTE: lowercase v is reserved for visual selection mode (batch operations,
   br-2bbt.10) which also applies on the Timeline screen. Using CAPITAL V avoids this
   conflict. Capital V is mnemonic for "View mode" and follows the pattern of Capital J
   for JSON toggle and Shift+T for theme cycling.

3. Commit data source:
   - Query git log from archive repositories
   - Parse commit messages for sender, recipients, message IDs
   - Cache results (refresh on archive write events)
   - Cross-project: show commits from all project archives

4. Commit entry rendering:
   - Short SHA (colored), author, date, subject line
   - Sender -> Recipients flow indicator
   - File change stats (+insertions, -deletions)
   - Linkable: Enter on commit deep-links to Archive Browser (br-2bbt.15) at that path

5. Stats summary bar:
   - Total commits (today/week/all)
   - Total insertions / deletions
   - Unique authors count
   - Active projects count

6. Optional: Mermaid-style ASCII gitGraph using ftui_extras canvas
   - Simplified branch/merge visualization
   - Only if frankentui mermaid supports gitGraph format

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timeline screen has Events/Commits/Combined toggle (V key, CAPITAL V)
- Commits view shows git history with SHA, author, date, stats
- Combined view interleaves events and commits chronologically
- Stats summary bar shows aggregate commit metrics
- Enter on commit navigates to Archive Browser
- Cross-project commit aggregation works
- v (lowercase) still works for visual selection mode (no conflict)
- Unit tests for git log parsing, commit data aggregation
- Snapshot tests for commit entry rendering
- E2E test: create messages (which create archive commits), switch to Commits view, verify commits shown","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:09:32.930138037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T02:14:41.672057742Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["archive","commits","timeline","tui","webapp-parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2bbt.16","depends_on_id":"br-2bbt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:09:32.930138037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -856,10 +856,10 @@
 {"id":"br-3h13.5.1","title":"Add HTTP dispatch error path tests: malformed requests, oversized body, bad Content-Type","description":"Server lib.rs has 248 tests but needs HTTP-level error paths NOT already covered by test_malformed_rpc.sh (327 lines, 15 cases — already tests invalid JSON, wrong JSON-RPC version, missing method, unknown method, missing id, batch requests, null params, etc.). Focus on HTTP-transport-level errors only: request with invalid Content-Type header, request with body exceeding max size, request with Content-Length mismatch (declared vs actual), request with HTTP method other than POST for tool calls (GET/PUT/DELETE), connection close mid-request (if detectable), empty body with Content-Type application/json, oversized headers. All with real HTTP server. Do NOT duplicate JSON-RPC validation already in test_malformed_rpc.sh. Target: 8+ new tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:41.902520420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:48:41.614168471Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.5.1","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:41.902520420Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-3h13.5.2","title":"Add auth edge case tests: expired JWT timing, JWKS rotation, RBAC role enforcement","description":"Server has JWT tests but needs edge cases. Add: JWT with exp exactly at current time (boundary), JWT with nbf in future (not-before), JWT with iat in future (issued-at), JWKS key rotation (old key→new key, verify old tokens rejected), JWKS endpoint returning HTTP 500 (fallback behavior), RBAC with reader role attempting write tool (should deny), RBAC with writer role on read tool (should allow), RBAC with unknown role, bearer token vs JWT priority (both present), auth bypass when http_allow_localhost_unauthenticated=true. Target: 12+ new tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.105941269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.105941269Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.5.2","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.105941269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-3h13.5.3","title":"Add rate limiting unit tests: burst overflow, per-tool limits, sliding window edges","description":"Rate limiting needs dedicated UNIT tests. NOTE: test_rate_limit.sh (410 lines, 5 E2E cases) already tests basic allow/deny, per-tool isolation, forwarded headers, JWT identity isolation at the HTTP level. Focus unit tests on internal rate-limiter MATH not testable via E2E: sliding window boundary precision (requests at exact window rollover), burst allowance accounting (verify token bucket math), per-tool limit independent of global limit, atomic counter under concurrent access (spawn 10 tasks), rate limit state after TTL expiry (verify cleanup), rate limit header values (X-RateLimit-Remaining calculation accuracy), shed decision threshold (verify shed_request returns correct health level at boundary). Target: 10+ new tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.309942914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:41:59.882755151Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.5.3","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.309942914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-3h13.5.4","title":"Add thin server module tests: disk_monitor(3), tool_metrics(3), cleanup(5), ack_ttl(7)","description":"Several server modules have very thin test coverage. disk_monitor(3): add tests for crossing warning/critical thresholds, rapid fluctuation, unmounted path handling. tool_metrics(3): add per-tool latency tracking accuracy, concurrent metric updates, histogram bucket correctness. cleanup(5): add cleanup with active reservations (skip vs clean), cleanup with locked files, cleanup dry-run mode. ack_ttl(7): add ack escalation timing, multiple escalation levels, ack with clock skew, ack for deleted message. Target: 20+ new tests total.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.513763040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.513763040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.513763040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Target: 20+ new tests total.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.513763040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:15:43.081084605Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.5.4","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:12:42.513763040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-3h13.6","title":"[track] Unit Test Deepening: Share & CLI Crates","description":"Close unit test gaps in mcp-agent-mail-share and mcp-agent-mail-cli. Share gaps: hosting(2 tests), snapshot(4), scope(7), scrub(8) — need: encryption roundtrip (encrypt→decrypt→verify), bundle corruption detection (truncated zip, invalid manifest hash, missing chunk), scrub edge cases (nested secrets, base64-encoded tokens, multi-line keys), deploy config generation for each platform. CLI gaps: command parsing edge cases (ambiguous subcommands, flag conflicts, --json combined with --verbose), output formatting (pipe mode vs TTY, unicode column alignment, very long values), error message quality tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:10:13.212107586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:10:13.212107586Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.6","depends_on_id":"br-3h13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:10:13.212107586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Target: 10+ new tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:13:01.527438631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:13:02.136270871Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T06:13:02.136251835Z","close_reason":"Added 11 CLI output formatting tests; compile blocked by unrelated robot.rs errors","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3h13.6.4","depends_on_id":"br-3h13.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:13:01.527438631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":374,"issue_id":"br-3h13.6.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Progress: 11 CLI output formatting tests added

### New tests in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/output.rs`:

1. `pipe_mode_no_ansi_codes_in_table` - verifies no ANSI escape sequences in pipe mode
2. `json_mode_valid_json_structure` - validates JSON field names and types match struct
3. `unicode_columns_alignment_preserved` - tests Japanese/Unicode character column alignment
4. `very_long_values_handled` - 500-char values render without truncation or panic
5. `json_mode_array_output_valid` - validates array output structure
6. `error_output_format_non_tty` - error: prefix and no ANSI in non-TTY mode
7. `single_row_result_rendering` - single-row table structure validation
8. `many_rows_performance` - 1000-row render performance (<100ms budget)
9. `mixed_empty_and_filled_cells` - empty cells maintain column alignment
10. `special_characters_in_data` - paths with spaces, quotes, backslashes preserved
11. `json_nested_objects` - nested object serialization correctness

### Validation status:
- `rustfmt --check` passes on output.rs
- Compile blocked by unrelated errors in robot.rs (modified by another agent)
- Tests are syntactically correct and will pass once workspace compiles

### Test coverage:
- Pipe mode: no ANSI, no box-drawing
- JSON mode: valid structure, correct field names, nested objects, arrays
- Unicode: column alignment preserved
- Long values: no truncation, no panic
- Performance: 1000 rows < 100ms
- Edge cases: empty cells, special characters

- Verify reproduction command generation and failure-path handling.
- Validate deterministic output/exit semantics expected by automation consumers.

## Deliverable
A robust test suite that protects flake-triage correctness during ongoing evolution.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Includes comprehensive unit, integration, and end-to-end coverage for this task's behavior (happy path, failure path, and boundary conditions).
- Produces detailed, machine-readable run artifacts: structured JSON summary, per-step command traces, stdout/stderr capture, timing data, and environment metadata needed for deterministic reproduction.
- Logging is high-signal and explicit: each assertion failure reports scenario ID, input fixture, expected vs actual values, and a recommended next debug step.
- CI execution is deterministic (seed/time controls, explicit timeouts/retries, no hidden external dependencies) and failures are actionable without manual log spelunking.
- Results are wired into standard quality gates so regressions block merges.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:37.532567227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:51.841790226Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-shfc","depends_on_id":"br-32ax","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:26:17.894159514Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":201,"issue_id":"br-shfc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T2.6: Tests for Flake-Triage CLI

## What to test

### Unit tests
1. **Artifact parsing**: Create sample *.failure.json files in a temp dir, verify
   scan_artifacts() finds and parses them correctly
2. **Malformed artifact handling**: Verify scan_artifacts() skips invalid JSON files
   with a warning (not a panic)
3. **Grouping**: Verify artifacts are grouped by test_name correctly
4. **Flake rate computation**: Given known pass/fail counts, verify flake_rate and
   is_flaky are correct
5. **Edge cases**: 0 seeds, 1 seed, all pass, all fail, empty directory

### Integration tests
6. **CLI flag parsing**: Verify all three subcommands accept their respective flags
7. **Scan with test fixtures**: Create a temp dir with fixture artifacts, run
   `am flake-triage scan --dir <tmp> --json`, verify JSON output structure
8. **Detect with a known-passing test**: Run detect against a test that always passes,
   verify is_flaky=false
9. **JSON output schema**: Verify all JSON outputs have consistent field naming

### Considerations
- Reproduce and detect tests require cargo test to be available. Mark these as
  #[ignore] for CI environments without a Rust toolchain, or use a mock Command.
- For scan tests, create fixture artifacts inline (no external file dependencies).

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/flake_triage.rs (mod tests)
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/tests/flake_triage_integration.rs
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:29:39Z"}]}

 {"id":"br-sk3y","title":"T7.1: Build parity matrix for current Python-based share wizard behavior and failure modes","description":"## Objective
Create a rigorous parity inventory for the existing Python wizard behavior so the Rust rewrite can be verified against explicit expectations rather than ad-hoc memory.

## Work
- Audit `run_share_wizard_in_cwd` resolution and subprocess behavior in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs`.
- Enumerate all current user-visible outputs (prompts, warnings, failure messages, exit codes).
- Capture file/path assumptions (`scripts/share_to_github_pages.py`, legacy tree fallback).
- Identify edge conditions: missing Python, missing script, non-source-tree invocation.

## Why it matters
This prevents accidental regressions and gives future maintainers a canonical parity baseline.

## Deliverable
Parity matrix document embedded in bead comments/description with explicit "must keep / intentional change" decisions.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Defines explicit inputs, outputs, invariants, error taxonomy, and exit-code behavior for downstream implementation.
- Captures parity mapping to legacy behavior, including intentional deviations and technical rationale.
- Includes a concrete validation strategy (unit + integration + e2e) and detailed logging/artifact expectations for downstream tasks.
- Is self-contained and executable without referring back to external markdown plan documents.
- Leaves no ambiguous requirements that would permit incompatible implementations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:00.311977531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:52:12.110473365Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:52:12.110453418Z","close_reason":"Completed parity matrix documenting script resolution flow, user-visible outputs, exit codes, edge conditions, PYTHONPATH handling, test coverage, and decisions for native implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","share","wizard"],"comments":[{"id":371,"issue_id":"br-sk3y","author":"ChartreuseRobin","text":"## Share Wizard Parity Matrix

### Overview
The `am share wizard` command delegates to a Python script (`share_to_github_pages.py`) that implements the interactive deployment wizard. The Rust code in `lib.rs` handles script resolution, Python execution, and exit code passthrough.

### Script Resolution Flow
The wizard script is resolved in priority order:
1. `{cwd}/scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` (in-bundle script)
2. `{source_root}/scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` (repo scripts dir)
3. `{source_root}/legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code/mcp_agent_mail/scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` (legacy fallback, only when running from source tree)

### Key Functions (lib.rs lines 4316-4433)
- `resolve_share_wizard_script(cwd)` → ShareWizardScriptResolution
- `run_share_wizard_in_cwd(cwd)` → orchestrates resolution and execution
- `run_python_script_in_cwd(script, cwd, pythonpath)` → Python subprocess
- `is_readable_file(path)` → existence + read check

### User-Visible Outputs
| Scenario | Output | Stream |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Wizard start | `Launching deployment wizard...` | stdout |
| Script not found | `Wizard script not found.` | stderr |
| Script not found | `Expected locations: {paths}` | stderr |
| Script not found | `This command only works when running from source.` | stderr |
| Script not found | `Run the wizard directly: python scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` | stderr |
| Python missing | Falls back to `python3` command | - |
| Success/Failure | Python script output passthrough | stdout/stderr |

### Exit Codes
| Code | Condition |
|------|-----------|
| 0 | Python script exits successfully |
| 1 | Script not found |
| 1 | Python/python3 command not found |
| N | Passthrough from Python script exit code |

### Edge Conditions
1. **Missing Python**: If `python` fails with ENOENT, retries with `python3`
2. **Missing script**: Fails with exit code 1 and detailed error message
3. **Non-source-tree invocation**: Legacy fallback only available when cwd is within source tree
4. **Legacy PYTHONPATH**: When using legacy script, adds `legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code/mcp_agent_mail/src` to PYTHONPATH
5. **Exit code passthrough**: Python exit code passed through unchanged (uses `.code().unwrap_or(1)`)

### PYTHONPATH Handling
When using the legacy script path, the PYTHONPATH is augmented:
- Prepends `{repo_root}/legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code/mcp_agent_mail/src`
- Preserves existing PYTHONPATH entries

### Existing Test Coverage (lib.rs)
1. `share_wizard_prints_guidance_when_script_not_found` - verifies error messages
2. `share_wizard_resolves_when_running_from_repo_root` - verifies resolution
3. `share_wizard_runs_stub_script_and_passthrough_exit_code` - verifies exit code passthrough

### Decisions for Native Implementation
| Behavior | Keep/Change | Rationale |
|----------|-------------|-----------|
| Python delegation | **CHANGE** | Native Rust implementation removes Python dependency |
| Exit code mapping | **KEEP** | Existing consumers expect these codes |
| Error messages | **KEEP** | Maintain UX consistency |
| Script resolution | **CHANGE** | No longer needed once native |
| PYTHONPATH handling | **REMOVE** | Not applicable to native |

### Validation Strategy
1. **Unit tests**: Test resolution logic, error message formatting
2. **Integration tests**: Test with mock bundle directories
3. **E2E tests**: End-to-end wizard flow with actual deployment targets

### Native Rust Implementation Notes
The native implementation should:
- Implement the same interactive prompts as the Python wizard
- Support GitHub Pages and Cloudflare Pages targets
- Emit the same user-visible progress messages
- Preserve exit code semantics
- Add structured JSON output mode (`--json`)

### Logging/Artifact Expectations
- Wizard progress should be logged to stderr
- Final result should be logged with structured info
- For `--json` mode, emit JSON to stdout with:
  - `success: bool`
  - `target: string` (github-pages, cloudflare-pages)
  - `url: string` (deployed URL)
  - `bundle_path: string`
  - `error: string` (if failed)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:52:06Z"}]}
